steps.title: Use the agency clue
steps.content: Reed says historians present political, business, and labor leaders as the major actors while ordinary workers are nearly devoid of agency. The blank must mean that historians have treated rank-and-file workers as peripheral or unimportant.

steps.title: Reject opposite meanings
steps.content: Choice A, "epitomize," would make workers perfect examples of something rather than downplayed actors. Choice C, "extol," means praise highly. Choice D, "substantiate," means support with evidence. The word for treating people as unimportant is "marginalize." The answer is B.
